scope.summary: Implement the bidirectional 2D-to-3D authority feedback loop: after each solver run, extract per-edge simulated arc-lengths from SolverResult and write them back to the GarmentSpec seam length annotations in tailor_panels, enabling the 2D editor to display actual draped edge lengths alongside authored lengths
acceptance_criteria: SolverResult carries a per-seam-edge simulated_arc_length_cm: Vec<f32> field for each seam id
After simulate() completes, solver_binding.rs writes simulated arc-lengths to tailor_panels as a non-authoritative annotation field (not altering the authored EdgeShape geometry)
The 2D editor displays both authored length and simulated length per seam edge; discrepancy triggers an advisory SEAM_LENGTH_DRIFT warning when delta > 5%
A model can read simulated arc-lengths from the SimulationReceipt to self-correct gather_ratio values
The write-back does not alter the authoritative EdgeShape coordinates (it is a read-only annotation on the authority row)
TailorSimRunCompleted event includes the arc-length annotation so EventLedger replay reconstructs it
scope.risk_if_missed: MOAT-1 is explicitly identified in the MD feature map as the core authoring moat. Without solver-to-2D arc-length feedback, a model cannot know whether its authored seam lengths match the actual draped geometry. Gather ratios are set blind, leading to over-gathered or under-gathered seams that require manual iteration. The SimulationReceipt self-correction loop (MT-268) becomes structurally incomplete: it can detect NO_INTERPENETRATION and DRAPE_CONVERGED failures but cannot guide seam-length corrections.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
